diagnosis: Same dual-panel chart structure with matching data points and trends. Key differences: legend in generated image is positioned at bottom-left and overlaps the x-axis label 'Epoch', whereas reference has legend at top-left cleanly separated. Generated uses different marker styles (plus signs, x marks visible in legend) and thinner lines. Data labels in left panel overlap more in generated version. Overall layout and values match but legend placement and marker rendering differ noticeably.

result.vision_prompt_fix: Always specify the exact legend position (e.g., 'top-left inside plot area', 'north west anchor') and confirm it does NOT overlap axis labels; describe marker shapes per series using concrete names (circle, square, triangle-up, diamond, triangle-down, plus, x) and line thickness (thin/medium/thick).
result.code_system_fix: When placing legends in pgfplots, use `legend pos=north west` (or matching corner) and ensure legend does not overlap x-axis label by adding `legend style={at={(0.02,0.98)}, anchor=north west, font=\small}`; use distinct mark types per series from {*, square*, triangle*, diamond*, triangle*, +, x, pentagon*} and set `line width=1.2pt` for visibility; for multi-series labeled data points, use `nodes near coords` with `every node near coord/.append style={font=\scriptsize, /pgf/number format/fixed}` and offset overlapping labels with `yshift` to prevent collisions.
result.root_cause: Vision description omitted precise legend anchor position and per-series marker styles, causing generated code to default to bottom-left legend overlapping axis label and use generic plus/x markers instead of filled geometric shapes.
